old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-da2q.1","title":"Spec: Multi-channel plan and coordination","description":"Define multi-channel plan and coordination protocol.

## Location
Add to spec/02-physical-link.md

## Channel plan

### Control channel (CH0)
- All routing: Announces, DIO, DIS, DAO
- Beacons (if TDMA)
- All nodes MUST listen on CH0 when idle

### Data channels (CH1-N)
- Application traffic only
- Node selects channel per-packet or per-flow

## Coordination

### Method 1: Hash-based (stateless)
data_ch = 1 + (hash(src_iid ^ dst_iid) mod (N_CHANNELS - 1))
- Both endpoints compute same channel
- No coordination needed
- Works for unicast only

### Method 2: Rendezvous (announced)
- Announce includes RX_CHANNEL field
- Sender uses destination's announced RX channel
- Allows per-node channel preference

### Method 3: Gateway-assigned
- Gateway assigns channels to nodes in DIO
- Load balancing across channels

## Regional parameters
- EU868: 8 channels (868.1-868.5, 867.1-867.9)
- US915: 64 uplink + 8 downlink channels
- Channel list in regional config, not hardcoded","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T18:21:20.687034Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T18:21:20.687034Z","labels":["spec","phy","scalability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-da2q.1","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-da2q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T18:21:20.837934Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value:
{"notes":"## Backwards Compatibility

**No flag day required.**

- Old nodes: Stay on CH0 (current single-channel behavior)
- New nodes: Use CH0 for control, data channels for application traffic
- Mixed network: CH0 carries ALL traffic types for old nodes

**Requirements:**
- CH0 MUST remain the control channel AND fallback data channel
- Old nodes never leave CH0, so all traffic TO old nodes MUST be on CH0
- New nodes MUST listen on CH0 for routing (announces, DIO)
- New nodes MAY use data channels for traffic between new nodes only
- Gateway MUST receive on all channels (or round-robin scan)

**Channel selection logic:**
if dst is old_node or dst.rx_channel unknown:
  use CH0
else:
  use dst.rx_channel (from announce) or hash-based

**Degradation:**
- Traffic to/from old nodes uses CH0 only
- New-to-new traffic uses data channels
- Capacity scales with fraction of new nodes"}
